column12: To be perfectly honest, it looks pretty much identical to the refinement 1 version of this candidate. Everything still works and it still has the same audios. Funnily enough ignored part of my prompt to change from emojis to actual images.
column13: Code-wise looks very similar, however it added a markdown file as a checklist for itself?

column12: It works, and the UI follows the standards of replacing the emojis with images in an AI theme. The spinning works for a bit then reverts to rapid switching. Small thing is that the icons will sort of "bounce" when matching, but slight error where the wrong ones bounce if a match of 2.
column13: Similar to its refinement 1, it keeps it relative structured and separated with comments.

column12: It also works the same as before, with the new images in an AI theme. The animations are pretty simple and it also has audio. It does however, have an issue with some of the token calculations, but it may be fixable.
column13: It is also pretty similar to its refinement 1, keeping the relative structure with comments.
